compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Completed and landed. Gerrit change 860 MERGED to main as commit d6c6da576 (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1). Evidence:
- det_citation.py (Layer-1 advisory both-encoding edge check + fail-closed reverse lookup); det_floor P6 advisory-lane wiring (never blocks); G1G2/E4/E6 Layer-2 show_ticket coverage protocol + fail-closed clause; author guidance in writing-a-passing-plan.md.
- Tests: 8 new (test_det_citation.py + test_citation_rubric_contract.py) + full plan-review regression (188) pass; ruff + mypy clean via pre-commit hook and CI Verified +1.
- [operator-attested] behavioral-eval AC: fixture created at docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/citation-grounding-coverage.md (two-plan scenario, N=10/>=80% threshold); live LLM eval is operator-run and deferred per the fixture — no in-session LLM eval was executed.

compiled_state.description:
## Problem
The plan-review code-grounding finders (G1G2/E4/E6) ground assertions ONLY against the origin/main snapshot and never consider the ticket's prerequisites, so a plan that relies on a symbol/file a prerequisite ticket will create is flagged "missing/unverifiable" and BLOCKED. G1G2 already SAYS "a symbol created by a ticket this ticket depends_on is treated as EXISTING" (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_G1G2.md:10`) but that rule is PROSE ONLY — unwired.

## Approach & rationale (G6/T1) — advisory det signal + LLM agent grounding; no new floor block
Introduce a machine-parseable inline citation `<subject> [rebar:<ticket-id>]` and honor it in two layers WITHOUT adding a blocking check to the DET floor (which is architecturally FAIL-OPEN per the det_floor docstring). No novel architecture (T1): reuses the `det_operator_attested` inline-tag PARSING approach (imported as a MODULE at `det_floor.py:452` via `from . import det_operator_attested`, called `det_operator_attested.ac_item_lines(...)` — NOT a regex-constant import) and the existing `show_ticket` reviewer tool (`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py`).

### Layer 1 — deterministic, ADVISORY (genuinely mirrors det_operator_attested, which never blocks)
New `det_citation.py` parses `[rebar:<id>]` tokens and verifies each cited id is a DIRECT UPSTREAM prerequisite of the plan ticket P. "Upstream" has TWO encodings and BOTH count as verified:
- (a) P declares the dependency: P's own `ctx.state["deps"]` contains `{relation:"depends_on", target_id:<cited>}`; OR
- (b) the cited ticket declares the block: the CITED ticket C's deps contains `{relation:"blocks", target_id:P}` (a reverse lookup — C blocks P ⇒ C is upstream of P).
Note (per the D2 review's correct catch): in a ticket's own deps, a `blocks` entry means THAT ticket blocks its target (target is DOWNSTREAM), so P's own `blocks` entries do NOT verify — only P.depends_on(C) or C.blocks(P) do. `det_citation` therefore takes P's deps PLUS an injected `resolve_deps(ticket_id) -> list[dep]` callable (det_floor supplies one backed by `_reads.show_ticket`) to do the bounded reverse lookup (one read per distinct cited id). A citation matching NEITHER (a) nor (b) → an ADVISORY coaching finding ("add a depends_on edge to <id>, or ensure <id> blocks this ticket, or remove the citation"). Never blocks (respects fail-open floor). `accruing_since` for any emitted signal is a module-level constant matching the existing reader modules.

### Layer 2 — LLM agent grounding (deliberately non-deterministic; blocking stays where it already lives)
The coverage-check is INTENTIONALLY an LLM judgment, NOT a string/path match — an exact matching rule is impractical because relied-upon functionality is described in prose, not verbatim symbols. Protocol (fully specified even though the judgment is non-deterministic — this is the same LLM decision every Pass-1 finder already makes):
- TRIGGER: a grounding finder (G1G2/E4/E6) is about to flag a symbol/functionality as missing, and that symbol is cited `<subject> [rebar:<C>]` with an edge VERIFIED by Layer 1.
- PROCEDURE: the finder retrieves C via the `show_ticket(C)` tool and judges whether C's plan/file_impact establishes the SPECIFIC functionality this finding claims is missing.
- INPUTS: the cited ticket C's content (plan + file_impact) and the specific missing-functionality claim under evaluation.
- DECISION (FAIL-CLOSED): credit the citation — treat the symbol as EXISTING and DROP the finding — ONLY if the reviewer affirmatively confirms C provides that functionality. If C does not, or the reviewer is uncertain, the finding STANDS. An uncited citation, an edge-unbacked citation (Layer 1), or an unconfirmed-coverage citation is ground-as-normal → still fails closed. Determinism is neither required nor claimed; what is specified is the protocol contract (trigger, procedure, inputs, decision, default).

Anti-bypass WITHOUT a new floor block: crediting requires BOTH a Layer-1-verified edge AND affirmative LLM coverage; a fabricated or coverage-absent citation earns no credit, so the normal missing-symbol block applies.

## Grammar (vocabulary — T8)
`<subject> [rebar:<ticket-id>]`: `<subject>` is free text naming the relied-upon code element (file path, module, class, function, or config key); the TOKEN is the trailing `[rebar:<ticket-id>]`; `<ticket-id>` matches rebar's id/alias grammar. det_citation parses the bracket; the subject is the human/LLM-readable referent.

## Grounding (E4/G1G2 — verified via Grep/Read)
- CONFIRMED `det_floor.py:452` = `from . import det_operator_attested`; it is ADVISORY ("p6 never blocks", `det_floor.py:486`).
- CONFIRMED `PlanContext.state` carries the show_ticket state incl. `deps` (`det_floor.py:104-126`); dep entries are `{target_id, relation}` from the ticket's outgoing perspective (D2 review-confirmed).
- CONFIRMED `show_ticket` is always in the reviewer tool set (`src/rebar/llm/pai_tools.py`).
- Rubric SoT: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_{G1G2,E4,E6}.md`. `docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md` is GENERATED (regenerate, never hand-edit).

## Scope
IN: `[rebar:<id>]` grammar; Layer-1 `det_citation.py` (advisory, both-direction edge verify via P.deps + resolve_deps reverse lookup) wired into det_floor's advisory lane; Layer-2 rubric edits (G1G2/E4/E6) specifying the LLM show_ticket coverage protocol; author guidance; regenerate the criteria guide.
OUT (separate): T8 overlay over-fire (bug 1a05-e3a8); snapshot-pinning; transitive-closure citations; deterministic coverage matching (explicitly rejected in favor of LLM judgment).

## Acceptance criteria (observable + proving command)
- [ ] `det_citation.parse_citations(plan_text)` returns `(subject, ticket_id)` per `[rebar:<id>]`. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_parse`.
- [ ] Edge-check verifies BOTH directions and rejects downstream: `{depends_on->C}` in P.deps → verified; `{blocks->P}` in resolve_deps(C) → verified; a `{blocks->X}` in P.deps (downstream) or no edge → unbacked. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_edge_both_directions`.
- [ ] The det check is ADVISORY — never flips the verdict to BLOCK. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_advisory_never_blocks`.
- [ ] WIRING: det_floor invokes det_citation in its advisory lane — a PlanContext whose plan has an edge-unbacked `[rebar:<id>]` yields the advisory citation finding, verdict NOT BLOCK. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_det_floor_wires_citation`.
- [ ] Rubrics G1G2/E4/E6 carry the `[rebar:<id>]` rule with the LLM show_ticket coverage PROTOCOL (trigger/procedure/inputs/fail-closed decision) AND the explicit "uncited / edge-unbacked / coverage-unconfirmed still fails closed" clause. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py`.
- [ ] Author guidance added. PROVE: `grep -q "\[rebar:" docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md`.
- [ ] Criteria guide regenerated + plan-review suite green. PROVE: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry regenerate-criteria-guide && pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review -k "citation or contract"`.

## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_citation.py (new; advisory parse + both-direction edge verify via injected resolve_deps)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_floor.py (advisory-lane wiring; supply resolve_deps backed by _reads.show_ticket; small addition — det_floor is 746/800 LOC)
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_{G1G2,E4,E6}.md (the LLM coverage protocol + fail-closed clause)
- docs/writing-a-passing-plan.md, docs/plan-review-criteria-guide.md (regenerated)
- tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py (new), tests/unit/plan_review/test_citation_rubric_contract.py (new)

## Estimated scope: M

## Round-3 additions (clearing the INDETERMINATE no-verification findings)
- ERROR HANDLING (Layer 1, fail-closed): the reverse-lookup `resolve_deps(C)`/`show_ticket(C)` is wrapped so ANY exception (store/network/missing-ticket) is caught and the citation is treated as UNVERIFIED (advisory finding stands) — the exception NEVER propagates into the advisory lane. A failed reverse-lookup counts as 'unbacked' (safe), not 'abstain'.
- RATIONALE for the separate module + injected callable (G6): `det_floor.py` is 746/800 LOC (near the hard module-size cap), so the citation logic MUST live in a separate `det_citation.py` — this is the module-size policy, not gratuitous indirection. The `resolve_deps` callable is injected (not a hard `_reads` import inside det_citation) purely so the edge-check is unit-testable with a fake resolver.

## Additional acceptance criteria
- [ ] REVERSE-LOOKUP FAIL-CLOSED: when the injected `resolve_deps` raises, the citation is reported UNVERIFIED and NO exception escapes det_citation. PROVE: `pytest -q tests/unit/plan_review/test_det_citation.py::test_reverse_lookup_error_fails_closed`.
- [ ] RUBRIC BEHAVIORAL EVAL (runtime, not text-presence): a plan-review behavioral eval fixture under `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/` exercises the Layer-2 coverage protocol on TWO plans — one citing a verified+covered prerequisite (expect: symbol credited, no missing-symbol finding) and one citing an edge-unbacked or coverage-absent prerequisite (expect: finding STANDS) — run over N samples against a predeclared threshold (mirrors the det_operator_attested lexicon-eval self-gating precedent). PROVE: the eval harness run recorded on the ticket. [operator-attested] — the behavioral-eval evidence (sample pass-rate vs threshold) is recorded on the ticket, as LLM-behavioral effects are validated by the eval harness rather than a single deterministic unit assertion.

file_impact.reason: new: parse [rebar:<id>] tokens + verify upstream depends_on/blocks closure (mirrors det_operator_attested)
wire the deterministic citation-edge block; expose the ticket own deps closure
operable rule: edge-verified [rebar:<id>] symbol grounded via show_ticket; uncited fails closed
depends_on-citation escape analogous to resolve_symbol third-party escape
proving command targeting a cited prerequisite symbol is valid (subsumes taxonomy item 9)
author guidance for the [rebar:<id>] citation convention
new: rubric contains the tag + fail-closed clause

compiled_state.title: Plan-review: honor verified [rebar:<id>] cross-ticket citations so depends_on-created symbols ground instead of false-positive
